# Pull Request Template

## Description
**Provide a clear and concise description of what this PR accomplishes.**

## Type of Change
Please select the type of change this PR introduces:

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Hardware design improvement
- [ ] Firmware enhancement
- [ ] Build system improvement
- [ ] Other (please specify)

## Related Issues
**Link any related issues using keywords:**
- Fixes #(issue number)
- Closes #(issue number)
- Relates to #(issue number)

## Changes Made
**List the specific changes made in this PR:**
- 
- 
- 

## Testing Performed
**Describe the testing you've done:**

### Hardware Testing (if applicable)
- [ ] Tested on actual ESP32 Feather V2 hardware
- [ ] Verified AS7265x sensor readings
- [ ] Checked OLED display functionality
- [ ] Tested battery monitoring
- [ ] Validated button input

### Software Testing
- [ ] Code compiles successfully
- [ ] No compiler warnings
- [ ] Tested in Arduino IDE
- [ ] Tested with PlatformIO
- [ ] Serial output verified

### Documentation Testing
- [ ] Documentation builds correctly
- [ ] Links work properly
- [ ] Images display correctly

## Screenshots/Evidence
**If applicable, add screenshots, test results, or other evidence of your changes working.**

## Breaking Changes
**If this is a breaking change, describe what breaks and how users should adapt:**

## Checklist
- [ ] My code follows the project's coding standards
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have tested my changes thoroughly
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published

## Additional Notes
**Add any additional notes, concerns, or questions for reviewers:**

## License Agreement
By submitting this pull request, I confirm that:
- [ ] My contributions are made under the appropriate project licenses (MIT for firmware, CERN OHL-W for hardware, CC BY-SA for mechanical, CC BY for documentation)
- [ ] I have the right to submit this work under these licenses
